EXT. MOUNTAIN TRAIL - DAWN

A narrow trail winds up the mountainside. Mist clings to the pine trees. The sun has not yet crested the ridge.

MAYA hikes steadily, her boots crunching on gravel. A heavy pack sits on her shoulders. She pauses, pulls out a water bottle, and drinks deeply.

She checks her compass. Studies the terrain ahead. The trail narrows to a ledge above a steep drop.

MAYA takes a deep breath. She tightens her pack straps and continues forward, one careful step at a time.

The ledge opens onto a wider path. Below, a valley stretches out — rivers cutting through green fields, a village in the distance smoking with morning fires.

MAYA pulls out a worn map. She traces a route with her finger, comparing it to the landscape below. She marks a spot with a pencil and folds the map away.

She finds a flat rock and sits. Opens a small journal. Writes a few lines. Tears a page out and tucks it into a plastic sleeve.

The wind picks up. Clouds roll in from the west. MAYA looks up, reads the sky. She stands quickly, adjusts her pack, and picks up the pace.

The trail descends now, switchbacks carved into the mountainside. Loose stones shift under her boots. She catches herself on a rock face, steadying.

Below, the village grows closer. Smoke rises from chimneys. A dog barks. Children's voices carry up the mountain.

MAYA reaches the valley floor. She crosses a wooden bridge over a narrow stream. The water runs clear over smooth stones.

She follows a dirt road toward the village. An old man sits on a porch, watching her approach.

OLD MAN
You're the hiker they warned us about.

MAYA
Warned you?

OLD MAN
Coming from the north. They said the pass would be snowed in by now.

MAYA
It was. I came through before the storm hit.

OLD MAN
Lucky.

MAYA nods. She keeps walking. The village square opens before her — a fountain, a market, a church with a crooked steeple.

She sets her pack down by the fountain. Drinks from the spout. Catches her reflection in the water.

FADE OUT.
